mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and raise corporate borrowing costs, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate increases are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0 percent, markets price in imminent monetary tightening and wage-driven inflation, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances when UNRATE < 4.0, or signal loses predictive power after 2018.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and consumer prices signals demand-pull inflation that forces central banks to restrict liquidity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint high readings of RSAFS and CPIAUCSL fail to predict negative returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production increases corporate revenue expectations and supports equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during economic expansions.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth correlates with negative forward returns during expansion periods, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly boosts corporate earnings forecasts and risk appetite, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP revisions show zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward returns across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding 2.5 percent annually triggers expectations of aggressive monetary policy normalization, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 2.5 percent consistently precede positive forward returns, or the signal's hit rate drops below 45% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and stable unemployment indicate robust labor market expansion that supports consumer spending and equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and stable UNRATE produces negative forward returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction term is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts signal deteriorating construction demand and tighter credit conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: Negative HOUST growth correlates with positive forward returns during late-cycle periods, or predictive accuracy falls below random chance (50%).
